settings.lede: Aira isn't a clinical device pretending to be wellness, or a wellness product pretending to be science. It's a precise, research-grounded tool designed to support what your body already knows how to do: breathe better.

settings.title: Sleep quality
settings.caption: Deeper REM cycles, fewer night-time wakings.

settings.heading: Why nasal breathing matters
settings.body: When you breathe through your nose, your body does work that mouth breathing simply can't replicate. The nasal cavity warms, humidifies, and filters incoming air. It produces nitric oxide[1] — a molecule that improves oxygen uptake in the lungs by up to 18%. And it sets the nervous system on a different course: nasal breathing is associated with deeper sleep, better focus, and more efficient recovery.

settings.caption: Increase in oxygen uptake from nasal-produced nitric oxide vs. mouth breathing.

settings.heading: The cost of mouth breathing
settings.body: Chronic mouth breathing has been linked to disrupted sleep, lower daytime energy, dental issues, and reduced athletic performance. In one 2018 study published in the Journal of Clinical Sleep Medicine, participants who switched to nasal-only breathing reported significantly improved sleep quality after 30 days.[2]

settings.heading: What "nasal patency" actually means
settings.body:
Nasal patency is the medical term for how open your nasal airway is. People with low patency — narrow nasal valves, deviated septums, congestion from allergies — work harder to breathe through their nose. Many give up and switch to mouth breathing without realising it.

Improving nasal patency, even mechanically and externally, restores the body's preferred breathing pattern.[3]

settings.heading: What better breathing actually changes
settings.body: The downstream effects of improved nasal breathing are well-documented in sleep medicine and sports science: deeper REM cycles, reduced snoring, faster heart rate variability recovery after exercise, and improved daytime focus.

settings.heading: Why nasal breathing matters more than you think.

settings.title: Soft contact
settings.body: Skin-safe, hypoallergenic adhesive secures the strip across the bridge of your nose.

settings.title: Sustained dilation
settings.body: Two precision-engineered magnets create a gentle outward pull on the nasal walls, opening the airway without pinching.

settings.title: All-night flow
settings.body: The strip stays in place through workouts, sleep, and side-sleeping — keeping your airway open without active effort.

settings.heading: How Aira works.
settings.lede: Aira gently expands the external nasal valve — the narrowest point of your nasal airway — and holds it open so air can flow freely. The mechanism is mechanical, not pharmacological.
settings.honest_doesnt: replace medical treatment for sleep apnea, chronic congestion, or structural issues. If you suspect any of these, see your doctor.
